response:
# Perspective Audit — Mega-Menu Navigation With Hover-Only Submenus ## Step 1 — Escalated perspectives confirmed Escalation received from a11y-critic: | Perspective | Alarm | Reviewed here | |---|---|---| | Keyboard & Motor Access | HIGH | Yes | | Screen Reader & Semantic Structure | MEDIUM | Yes | | Cognitive & Neurodivergent | MEDIUM | Yes | Not escalated (checklists **not** run, per protocol): Magnification & Reflow, Environmental Contrast, Vestibular & Motion Sensitivity, Auditory Access. See the *Escalation calibration note* at the end — two of these show real defects in the source and look under-triaged by Layer 1. Scope note: the artifact is a React component fixture (no document shell). Page-level criteria that require ``/`` (2.4.2 title, 3.1.1 `lang`) and site-level criteria that require multiple pages (3.2.3, 3.2.4, 3.2.6, 2.4.5) cannot be confirmed from this source and are marked accordingly rather than scored. --- ## Step 4 — Per-perspective checklists ### Perspective 5 — Keyboard & Motor Access (HIGH) | # | Checklist item (WCAG) | Result | Evidence | |---|---|---|---| | 1 | All interactive elements reachable/operable by keyboard (2.1.1) | **FINDING (CRITICAL)** | Submenu render is guarded by `item.children.length > 0 && openMenu === item.id`; `openMenu` is set **only** by `onMouseEnter`. No `onFocus`/`onKeyDown`/`onClick`. Submenu links never enter the DOM for a keyboard user. → **KM-01** | | 2 | No keyboard traps (2.1.2) | PASS | Focus moves through the top-level `` elements normally; submenus never mount for keyboard, so no trap exists. | | 3 | Skip link present, targets main (2.4.1) | PASS | `` targets `
`; `.skip-link:focus` restores it to the layout (visible on focus). | | 4 | Focus order logical (2.4.3) | PASS | Order of focusable items: skip link → logo → Products → Solutions → Resources → Pricing → main. Logical. (Submenus are missing from focus order — that is a reachability defect, KM-01, not an ordering defect.) | | 5 | Focus indicator visible ≥2px, ≥3:1 (2.4.7; 2.4.13 is AAA) | PASS | `.nav-link:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #005fcc; outline-offset: 2px; }`; `.logo:focus` 3px; `.submenu-link:focus-visible` 2px + `#e3f2fd` fill. Clearly visible on every reachable control. | | 6 | Multi-point gestures have single-pointer alt (2.5.1) | N/A | No multi-point gestures. | | 7 | Activation on up-event, abortable (2.5.2) | PASS | Standard anchor activation (up-event). | | 8 | Visible label = accessible name (2.5.3) | PASS | Text links; visible text is the accessible name ("Products", "Widget Pro", …). | | 9 | Drag operations have click alternative (2.5.7) | N/A | No drag interactions. | | 10 | Targets ≥24×24 CSS px + spacing (2.5.8) | PASS | `.nav-link` padding 12×16 and `.submenu-link` padding 10×16 both yield target boxes well above 24px in each axis. | | 11 | Portrait + landscape (1.3.4) | N/A | No orientation lock. | **Red-flag scan:** "Interactive element unreachable by keyboard" → **FIRES** (KM-01, auto-CRITICAL). Keyboard trap → no. Drag-only → no. `outline:none` with no replacement → no (outlines present). --- ### Perspective 6 — Screen Reader & Semantic Structure (MEDIUM) | # | Checklist item (WCAG) | Result | Evidence | |---|---|---|---| | 1 | Landmarks present/correct (1.3.1) | PASS | `
`, `
`, `
`. `aria-label` correctly disambiguates the nav. | | 2 | Heading hierarchy, `

` present (2.4.6) | PASS | Single `

Welcome to Acme Corp

`, no skipped levels. | | 3 | Images have alt / decorative `alt=""` (1.1.1) | N/A | No ``; logo is a text link. | | 4 | DOM order matches visual order (1.3.2) | PASS | Submenu markup is emitted immediately after its trigger ``; absolute positioning is visual only. | | 5 | Unique descriptive `` (2.4.2) | N/A (out of fixture scope) | No document `<head>` in a component fixture — verify at page level. | | 6 | Link text describes destination (2.4.4) | PASS | All link text is self-describing in context. | | 7 | `lang` on `<html>` (3.1.1) | N/A (out of fixture scope) | No `<html>` in fixture — **not** scored as the "missing lang" red flag; verify at page level. | | 8 | Foreign phrases wrapped in `lang` (3.1.2) | N/A | All English. | | 9 | Inputs have associated labels (3.3.2) | N/A | No form inputs. | | 10 | Errors identify field + `aria-describedby` (3.3.1) | N/A | No forms/errors. | | 11 | Custom widgets use correct ARIA roles per APG (4.1.2) | **FINDING (MAJOR)** | Trigger links carry no `aria-expanded`, no `aria-haspopup`, no `aria-controls`; the expandable/collapsed state exists only as the visual hover effect. Nothing communicates "this item reveals a submenu" or its open/closed state to AT. → **SR-01** | | 12 | Status messages use `aria-live` appropriately (4.1.3) | PASS (correct absence) | A disclosure needs `aria-expanded` state change, **not** a live region. Absence of `aria-live` here is correct, not a defect — see *Avoided false alarms*. | | 13 | SPA route changes announced/focus-managed | N/A | Plain `<a href>` full-page navigations; no client routing in source. | **Red-flag scan:** No `<main>` → does not fire (main present). Unlabeled inputs → n/a. "Custom widget with no ARIA role" → **does not fire** — a navigation disclosure built from real `<a>` links is a valid APG approach; menu/menuitem roles are **not** required (see *Avoided false alarms*). "Missing `lang` on `<html>`" → not fired: out of fixture scope, flagged for page-level verification rather than auto-CRITICAL. --- ### Perspective 7 — Cognitive & Neurodivergent (MEDIUM) | # | Checklist item (WCAG) | Result | Evidence | |---|---|---|---| | 1 | Error messages suggest corrective action (3.3.3) | N/A | No forms. | | 2 | Multi-step forms don't re-ask (3.3.7) | N/A | No forms. | | 3 | Auth allows paste/autofill, no puzzle (3.3.8) | N/A | No auth. | | 4 | Nav in same relative position across pages (3.2.3) | N/A (needs multi-page) | Single component; conventional top-nav. Verify across pages. | | 5 | Same-function components labeled consistently (3.2.4) | N/A (needs multi-page) | Single instance. | | 6 | Help mechanism in consistent location (3.2.6) | N/A | No help mechanism present. | | 7 | Focus does not auto-trigger context change (3.2.1) | PASS | No `onFocus` handler; focusing a nav link changes nothing. (The reveal is bound to hover, not focus — that is the KM-01 defect, but it means 3.2.1 is not violated.) | | 8 | Changing a control value doesn't auto-submit/navigate (3.2.2) | N/A | No form controls. | | 9 | Destructive actions have undo/confirm (3.3.4) | N/A | No destructive actions. | | 10 | More than one way to reach a page (2.4.5) | N/A (site-level) | Top-level section links exist (`/products`, …); full-path redundancy is a site-level property. | | 11 | Personal-data inputs have `autocomplete` (1.3.5) | N/A | No inputs. | | 12 | Timeout warnings ≥20s (2.2.1) | N/A | No timeouts. | | 13 | Avoids unexplained jargon / complex sentences | PASS | Labels are plain and concrete ("Products", "Documentation", "Support"); product names are unavoidable and clear. | | ND-a | Predictable layout, no shift | PASS | Submenu is `position: absolute` and overlays rather than reflowing content; opening it does not shift the page. | | ND-b | Clear visual hierarchy | PASS | Header → nav → main with a single `<h1>`. | | ND-c | No autoplay / unprompted modals | PASS | No autoplay media; no modal dialogs. The submenu is user-initiated (hover), not unprompted. | | ND-d | Instructions don't rely on memory of prior steps | N/A | No multi-step flow. | **Red-flag scan:** CAPTCHA/cognitive test → none. Paste blocked → none. Redundant re-entry → none. Destructive action without confirmation → none. **No red flag fires.** **Cognitive verdict:** No independent CRITICAL/MAJOR at AA. The genuine cognitive concern for this component — top-level items give no visible cue that a submenu exists, and (for pointer users) the submenu vanishes the instant the pointer leaves, forcing repeated precise re-approach — has its clean WCAG home in **1.4.13 Content on Hover or Focus**, which belongs to the *Magnification & Reflow* perspective (not escalated). I am **not** manufacturing a cognitive-perspective citation for it; it is recorded in the calibration note below and its actionable fixes ride along with KM-01/SR-01. Escalating Cognitive to MEDIUM was reasonable as a discoverability signal, but on its own AA criteria this component passes. --- ## Step 5 — Findings **Finding (KM-01):** Submenu links are reachable only via `mouseenter`; there is no keyboard or focus path to open a submenu, so the child links (Widget Pro, Widget Lite, API Toolkit, Enterprise Suite, and the Solutions/Resources children) are never rendered for, or reachable by, keyboard-only users. What the user experiences: tabbing across the nav lands on the four top-level links and stops; every second-level destination behind the mega-menu is simply unavailable without a mouse. **WCAG:** 2.1.1 Keyboard (Level A) **Perspective:** Keyboard & Motor Access **Severity:** CRITICAL (red flag: interactive element unreachable by keyboard) **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: UX Design — the hover-only interaction pattern needs to become a focus/click-driven disclosure) **Evidence:** `MegaMenu` — `<li className="nav-item" onMouseEnter={() => setOpenMenu(item.id)} onMouseLeave={() => setOpenMenu(null)}>`; submenu guard `item.children.length > 0 && openMenu === item.id`. No `onFocus`, `onKeyDown`, `onClick`, or focus-within CSS anywhere in the component. **Fix:** Make each item with children a real disclosure: promote the trigger to a `<button aria-expanded aria-controls>` (or add keyboard/focus handling to the link), open the submenu on `click`/`Enter`/`Space` and on `focus`/`focus-within`, move focus into/through the submenu, and close on `Escape` and on focus-out. Rendering the submenu on focus (not only hover) is what puts the child links into the DOM and the tab order. --- **Finding (SR-01):** The top-level items that expand submenus expose no disclosure semantics — no `aria-expanded`, no `aria-haspopup`, no `aria-controls`. A screen-reader user hears "Products, link" with no indication a submenu exists and no open/closed state. Because the submenu is also hover-gated (KM-01), the child links are absent from the accessibility tree entirely, so the **net impact for AT users is total loss of the mega-menu's second level** — the SR manifestation of the same root defect. Fixing KM-01 (render on focus) is the prerequisite; SR-01 is the semantic layer that must land with it. **WCAG:** 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value (Level A); relationship dimension 1.3.1 Info and Relationships (Level A) **Perspective:** Screen Reader & Semantic Structure **Severity:** MAJOR (state/relationship not programmatically determinable; escalates to a total-loss barrier when combined with KM-01) **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: UX Design — defines the disclosure interaction) **Evidence:** `MegaMenu` — trigger is `<a href={item.href} className="nav-link">{item.label}</a>` with no ARIA state; submenu is `<div className="submenu">` of `<a>` links with no programmatic tie to the trigger. **Fix:** On the disclosure control, expose `aria-expanded="true|false"` reflecting the open state and `aria-controls` pointing at the submenu container's `id`; keep the submenu as real links (do **not** add menu roles — see below). The `aria-expanded` change is what AT announces on open/close; no `aria-live` is needed. --- ### Avoided false alarms (discernment record) These are surfaces a box-checking pass would flag; each is correct as built and is **not** a finding: - **Missing `role="menu"` / `role="menuitem"` on the submenu** (the source comment calls this a "BUG"). It is not. WAI-ARIA APG reserves `menu`/`menuitem` for application-style action menus with full arrow-key roving-tabindex semantics; for **site navigation**, a disclosure that reveals a plain list of links is the recommended, lower-risk pattern. Adding menu roles here would *introduce* defects (it would promise keyboard behaviors the component doesn't implement). The real gap is the disclosure state (SR-01), not the role. - **No `aria-live` on submenu open/close.** A disclosure communicates state through `aria-expanded` on its trigger, which AT announces automatically. Bolting on a live region would be redundant and noisy — the wrong tool. - **Focus indicators / skip link / contrast.** `:focus-visible` outlines are present and adequate on every reachable control (2.4.7 PASS), the skip link works, and nav/submenu text contrast (5.4:1, 12.6:1) is fine. No fabricated findings here. (Contrast formally belongs to *Environmental Contrast*, not reviewed.) --- ## Contract blocks — new CRITICAL/MAJOR findings ``` ### A11y Evidence Finding finding_id: hover-nav-km01-keyboard-unreachable-submenu fingerprint: 7f3c9a2e5b1d4066 source: perspective-audit Keyboard & Motor Access checklist (item 1; red flag: unreachable by keyboard) wcag_or_apg: WCAG 2.2 SC 2.1.1 Keyboard (Level A); WAI-ARIA APG Disclosure (Show/Hide) pattern section_508_fpc_context: In scope — Revised Section 508 (E207.2/602.3) incorporates WCAG 2.0 A/AA; 2.1.1 is WCAG 2.0 Level A severity: CRITICAL perspective_alarms: keyboard_motor=HIGH, screen_reader_semantic=MEDIUM (shared root cause) evidence: MegaMenu nav-item <li> uses onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave only; submenu render guarded by `openMenu === item.id`; no onFocus/onKeyDown/onClick; submenu links absent from DOM without a pointer hover reproduction_steps: Keyboard only — Tab through the header nav. Focus reaches Products/Solutions/Resources/Pricing and proceeds to main. No Enter/Space/Arrow/Esc opens any submenu; child links (e.g., Widget Pro) never receive focus and are not in the DOM. Confirmed by source review of the render guard. expected_behavior: Keyboard/focus opens the submenu; child links enter the tab order; Escape closes and returns focus to the trigger actual_behavior: Submenu opens on hover only; keyboard and AT users cannot reach any second-level link trend: new ``` ``` ### A11y Evidence Finding finding_id: hover-nav-sr01-missing-disclosure-semantics fingerprint: c4e18b7a2f9d3055 source: perspective-audit Screen Reader & Semantic Structure checklist (item 11) wcag_or_apg: WCAG 2.2 SC 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value (Level A); SC 1.3.1 Info and Relationships (Level A); WAI-ARIA APG Disclosure pattern section_508_fpc_context: In scope — WCAG 2.0 Level A (4.1.2, 1.3.1) incorporated by Revised Section 508 severity: MAJOR perspective_alarms: screen_reader_semantic=MEDIUM, keyboard_motor=HIGH (net total-loss when combined) evidence: Trigger is a plain <a className="nav-link"> with no aria-expanded/aria-haspopup/aria-controls; submenu <div className="submenu"> has no programmatic relationship to its trigger reproduction_steps: Source review — inspect nav-item trigger and submenu container for ARIA state/relationship attributes; none present. (Runtime AT confirmation blocked by KM-01, since the submenu never mounts without hover.) expected_behavior: AT announces the item as expandable with a collapsed/expanded state (aria-expanded), tied to the submenu via aria-controls actual_behavior: No expandable state or relationship is exposed; with hover-gating, the entire submenu is absent from the accessibility tree trend: new ``` --- ## Escalation calibration note (not scored here — flagged for Layer-1 re-triage) Protocol discipline means I did **not** run checklists for LOW perspectives. But two non-escalated perspectives show defects visible in the source, and silently passing them would be dead output: - **Vestibular & Motion (rated LOW).** `.submenu { animation: slideDown 0.2s ease; }` with a `translateY` keyframe and **no** `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` guard (the source even comments the omission). That is a textbook motion red flag ("`prefers-reduced-motion` absent when animations exist") and looks **MEDIUM**, not LOW. - **Magnification & Reflow (rated LOW).** `onMouseLeave` hides the submenu immediately with no grace period, and the content is not dismissible via `Escape` or reliably hoverable — the core of **1.4.13 Content on Hover or Focus**. This also underlies the cognitive discoverability concern above. Looks **MEDIUM**, not LOW. Recommend the orchestrator re-run Layer 1 on these two, or authorize a follow-up perspective pass. I have not assigned severities to them here. --- ## Step 6 — Perspective Audit Summary **Component/artifact reviewed:** Mega-Menu Navigation With Hover-Only Submenus (`MegaMenu` React fixture) **Perspectives escalated for deep review:** Keyboard & Motor Access (HIGH), Screen Reader & Semantic Structure (MEDIUM), Cognitive & Neurodivergent (MEDIUM) **Perspectives at LOW (not reviewed):** Magnification & Reflow, Environmental Contrast, Vestibular & Motion Sensitivity, Auditory Access — *see calibration note: Motion and Magnification/Reflow appear under-escalated.* ### Findings by Severity | Severity | Count | |---|---| | CRITICAL | 1 | | MAJOR | 1 | | MINOR | 0 | | ENHANCEMENT | 0 | ### Perspectives That Passed - **Cognitive & Neurodivergent** — all in-scope AA criteria PASS or N/A; no independent CRITICAL/MAJOR. (Discoverability/hover-persistence concern routed to 1.4.13 under the non-escalated Magnification & Reflow perspective — see calibration note.) ### Perspectives With Findings — Grouped by ARRM Role **Front-End Dev** - Submenu unreachable by keyboard (render + open bound to hover only) — Keyboard & Motor — CRITICAL — 2.1.1 Keyboard - Missing disclosure semantics (`aria-expanded`/`aria-haspopup`/`aria-controls`) — Screen Reader & Semantic — MAJOR — 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value (with 1.3.1) **UX Design** (secondary owner on both) - Redefine the interaction from hover-reveal to a focus/click disclosure (open on click/Enter/Space + focus, close on Escape/focus-out, grace period for pointer). Enables the Front-End fixes above. **Visual Design** — none. **Content Author** — none (link text is clear and descriptive). ### Open Questions Requiring Human Verification - Page-level `<title>` (2.4.2) and `<html lang>` (3.1.1) — not present in the component fixture; confirm at the document level. *[Human verification required — cannot be confirmed from source]* - Consistent navigation position/labeling and multiple paths to pages (3.2.3, 3.2.4, 2.4.5) — require the assembled multi-page site. *[Human verification required — cannot be confirmed from source]* ### Recommended Next Step **BLOCK** — one CRITICAL (KM-01, 2.1.1) makes the mega-menu's entire second level unavailable to keyboard and AT users. Do not ship until the submenu is reachable via keyboard/focus and the disclosure state is exposed (KM-01 + SR-01 fixed together), then re-test with `a11y-test` (real keyboard walkthrough + AT verification). Also re-triage the Motion and Magnification/Reflow perspectives per the calibration note before final sign-off.

_benchmark.mechanism: Claude Code Agent(general-purpose, model=opus), background, one per fixture
_benchmark.protocol_note: prompts composed identically to run_cloud_benchmark.py build_escalation_prompt EXCEPT fixture truncated before '## Accessibility Issues' (answer key withheld — prior lanes were non-blind)
